verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a soluble protein lacking a transmembrane domain. Primary evidence indicates it is cytoplasmic/nuclear (per NCBI/Protein Atlas), and characterized as intra-acrosomal in sperm. Unlike its family members IZUMO1-3, IZUMO4 does not possess the structural features required for direct cell-surface accessibility. While it participates in fertilization-related protein complexes during sperm biology, it engages soluble or compartmentalized partners rather than functioning as an extracellular-accessible receptor or membrane-anchored protein.
